summary: Machine policy for File History. Good for privacy-sensitive or storage-constrained setups, but it prevents regular automatic File History backups from being activated.

setting.name: File History
setting.casual_explanation: File History is Windows' built-in feature for making regular automatic backups of personal files. This policy decides whether File History can be turned on.
setting.why_people_change_it: People disable File History when they want to avoid local backup activity, reduce stored copies of personal files, or use a different backup strategy.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Turning it off reduces backup-related activity and stored file copies, but it also removes one of Windows' easiest built-in backup options.
setting.professional_notes: FileHistory.admx maps the machine policy to HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\FileHistory\Disabled with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0. The local ADML help text says enabling the policy prevents File History from being activated to create regular automatic backups.

allowed_values.label: Not configured
allowed_values.meaning: Windows does not force the File History policy.

allowed_values.label: File History allowed
allowed_values.meaning: The policy is disabled and File History can be activated for regular automatic backups.

allowed_values.label: File History disabled
allowed_values.meaning: File History cannot be activated to create regular automatic backups.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes Disabled = 1 under the File History policy key, which matches the documented disable behavior.

windows_defaults.label: Windows device-policy default
windows_defaults.applies_to: Windows 8 and newer systems supporting File History policy

states.rationale: The machine policy is not configured by default.

windows_defaults.notes: The local ADML help text says if the policy is disabled or not configured, File History can be activated to create regular automatic backups.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
intended_for: People who may want Windows' built-in automatic file backup feature
avoid_for: Users who explicitly do not want File History available
recommended_profiles.good_when: You want File History to stay available as a built-in backup option.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want to make sure File History cannot be activated on the device.
tradeoffs: File History remains available to be turned on.
No policy-based restriction is enforced for this backup feature.

recommended_profiles.label: No File History
intended_for: Devices using a different backup strategy
avoid_for: Users who rely on File History for automatic backup

states.rationale: Prevent File History from being activated for regular automatic backups.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want File History unavailable on the device.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want a simple built-in way to keep automatic file backups.
tradeoffs: The File History backup feature is blocked.
Users must rely on another backup method if they want automatic protection.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft FileHistory.admx mapping
evidence.summary: The local ADMX file maps the policy to HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\FileHistory\Disabled with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft FileHistory.adml help text
evidence.summary: The local ADML help text says enabling the policy prevents File History from being activated for regular automatic backups, while disabled or not configured leaves it available.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes Disabled = 1 under the File History policy key for the current tweak.

evidence.title: Existing tweak source record
evidence.summary: The repo already tracks provenance for this tweak in the current catalog output.

decision.why: Local Microsoft policy files document the exact File History policy surface, value semantics, and backup behavior. The app writes the documented disable value.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: <policy name="DisableFileHistory" class="Machine" ... key="Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\FileHistory" valueName="Disabled">; FileHistory.adml: If you enable this policy setting, File History cannot be activated to create regular, automatic backups.
validation_proof.notes: Local official ADMX defines the exact key and value name; local ADML confirms that enabling the policy prevents File History from being activated for regular automatic backups.
